title: coeloneurum ferrugineum
intro: Coeloneurum ferrugineum, a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia, is a member of the family Lamiaceae. Its leaves and stems are used in traditional medicine, known for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Coeloneurum ferrugineum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in topical applications to promote wound healing and skin health.
